Key entities include Funtap Games, core genres (SLG, RTS, Idle, Roguelike, MMO, Hybrid Casual, Puzzle, Slots), platforms (Android/iOS, TikTok, Steam), analytics provider 广大大 (GuangdaDa), and notable publishers/titles (Tencent, Moonton, Turkish studios, *Whiteout Survival*, *Black Myth: Wukong*, *Gossip Harbor*).\n\n**Dates:** Primary analytical window spans January–December 2025, with industry context referencing late 2020 (product model exploration), 2023 (testing/adjustment), 2024 (major releases), 2025 (current \"quality leap\"/strategic pivot), and 2026 (projected focus).\n\n**Definitions:** FTUE (First Time User Experience), \"honeymoon period\" (initial engagement phase), \"high-frequency, low-duration\" session model, \"social watershed\" (retention divergence point), \"10-hour rule\" (600 cumulative minutes in week one increases paying conversion probability by 5x), LTV, \"Adjustment\" phase, \"Traffic-driven\" strategy, and \"Numerical-value\" design model.\n\n**Procedures & Creative Formulas:** Details standardized ad sequences per genre: Strategy (high-altitude fall → number growth/parkour → clearance → intentional failure); RPG (AI intro → item showcase → battles → failure → customization → combo); Simulatio..."

message.content:
This section (p. 51) delivers a retrieval-optimized synthesis of 2025 global mobile game marketing trends, focusing on genre-specific user acquisition (UA) strategies, early-stage performance validation, and cross-market industry expansion. 

**Topics & Entities:** Covers UA and creative trend analysis across Strategy, RPG, Simulation, Casual, Puzzle, and Casino/Entertainment genres, alongside Chinese publisher overseas expansion dynamics. Key entities include Funtap Games, analytics provider 广大大 (GuangdaDa), platforms (Android/iOS, TikTok, Steam), and notable publishers/titles (Tencent, Moonton, Turkish studios, *Whiteout Survival*, *Black Myth: Wukong*, *Gossip Harbor*).

**Dates:** Primary analytical window spans January–December 2025, with industry context referencing late 2020 (product model exploration), 2023 (testing/adjustment), 2024 (major releases), 2025 (current strategic pivot), and 2026 (projected focus).

**Definitions & Metrics:** Defines FTUE (First Time User Experience), "honeymoon period," "high-frequency, low-duration" session model, "social watershed," LTV, "Adjustment" phase, "Traffic-driven" strategy, and "Numerical-value" design model. Highlights the "10-hour rule" metric (600 cumulative minutes in week one increases paying conversion probability by 5x), alongside retention benchmarks and scalability readiness indicators.

**Procedures & Claims:** Details standardized, genre-specific ad creative sequences (e.g., Strategy: high-altitude fall → number growth/parkour → clearance → intentional failure; RPG: AI intro → item showcase → battles → failure → customization → combo) and outlines procedures for early metric validation and creative optimization. Claims emphasize a strategic industry shift toward quality-driven UA, early retention validation, and structured creative testing.

**Evidence Types & Visuals:** Draws on industry analytics, creative trend breakdowns, and publisher case studies. No specific tables or figures are detailed in the source text. (Note: Associated child node 0127 is a non-substantive section header with no additional data.)
